scope: CFS UI optimization, dashboard cleanup, fullscreen map behavior, SceneView performance tuning, and methodology transparency
sceneview_optimization_changes: Configured SceneView as a Cabarrus-focused local scene instead of an unconstrained global scene.
Added a Cabarrus study extent in src/lib/gis/gisConfig.ts.
Applied SceneView clippingArea from the Cabarrus study extent.
Constrained SceneView altitude and tilt to reduce excessive global rendering behavior.
Reduced expensive visual effects by disabling atmosphere, stars, and direct shadows.
Reduced SceneView qualityProfile from high to medium for prototype responsiveness.
Preserved selected parcel focus, parcel cage, development hotspots, flood constraints, and FEMA flood zones.
data_request_optimization_changes: Closed Due Diligence collapsed sections now lazy-render their contents, reducing hidden panel API calls.
Overview no longer renders full countywide parcel/development/flood metric panels.
Developer-only temporal, diagnostics, scenario, and report-preview surfaces remain hidden unless NEXT_PUBLIC_CFS_DEVELOPER_MODE=true.
FEMA Flood Zones default limit changed from 500 polygons to 100 polygons.
Visible-extent FEMA flood zone fallback also uses 100 polygons when no extent is available.
No backend API contracts or PostGIS schemas were changed.
left_panel_layer_control_cleanup: Left map layer rail default desktop width increased to 336px.
Added desktop drag handle so the map layer rail can be resized between 292px and 430px.
Map Layers remains collapsed by default to reduce initial visual noise.
Layer status chips were redesigned as wrapping readable badges instead of tiny truncated uppercase labels.
Development Hotspots, Flood Constraints, FEMA Flood Zones, placeholder layers, and existing toggles were preserved.
fullscreen_map_behavior_changes: Fullscreen map mode keeps the left layer rail available.
Fullscreen map mode hides the right intelligence panel.
Fullscreen map mode hides the bottom county KPI strip.
Escape still exits fullscreen map focus mode.
Panel state is preserved when returning from fullscreen.
right_overview_intelligence_cleanup: Overview mode now focuses on selected parcel snapshot and next action.
Removed the heavy county pulse insight block from the right Overview rail.
Detailed parcel, development, flood, and school panels remain in Due Diligence or supporting collapsed sections.

Selected parcel Overview summary remains concise with parcel ID, owner/account, neighborhood/subdivision, zoning, FEMA review status, latest permit activity, and links to Due Diligence/Executive Print.
bottom_kpi_cleanup: Bottom KPI row remains limited to four compact county pulse indicators.
Reduced footer padding, icon size, card padding, and value text size.
KPI strip is hidden in fullscreen map mode and Executive Print mode.

documents: Parcels, zoning, permit/development activity, FEMA NFHL flood constraints, school attendance-zone assignment, and presentation-derived school utilization seed.
Flood constraints are regulatory FEMA polygon-to-parcel overlays.
School assignment uses attendance-zone polygon overlap, not school point distance.
Permit segmentation and hotspot concentration are descriptive signals.
Prediction, official school capacity scoring, and final infrastructure readiness scoring are not active.

removed_or_reworked_redundant_ui: Overview no longer duplicates full countywide metric panels already represented by the bottom KPI row or detailed sections.
Map layer status text no longer appears as unreadable one-letter/tiny chips.
Full detail panels are tucked into Due Diligence collapsed sections rather than appearing in the default Overview.

validation_results.backend_validation: not run; backend files were not changed for this task

local_runtime_check.localhost_3000: not reachable during final check; dev server was not running
local_runtime_check.fastapi_health: not reachable during final check; backend dev server was not running
local_runtime_check.dev_cfs_launcher: not run because launcher scripts were not changed

remaining_known_limitations: ArcGIS performance still depends on browser GPU, local machine resources, and ArcGIS asset loading.
FEMA polygon rendering remains capped/extent-filtered for prototype safety.
Some placeholder readiness layers remain intentionally mock/static.
Official school capacity scoring is not active until verified enrollment/capacity data is ingested.
A live browser smoke test was not completed because the local dev stack was not running during final validation.
recommended_next_step: Start the local dev stack with npm run dev:cfs and visually smoke test Overview, Methodology, fullscreen map mode, selected parcel flow, Development Hotspots, Flood Constraints, FEMA Flood Zones, and Due Diligence.
